perturbation.original_text: 1. EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY DUTIES AND PROVISIONS OF GOVERNMENT CONTRACTORS In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.4(a), the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by that section, including but not limited to, Contractor's agreement that it will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The Contractor will take affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during employment, without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous oral or written representation with regard to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties. EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY DUTIES AND PROVISIONS OF GOVERNMENT CONTRACTORS In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.4(a), the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by that section, including but not limited to, Contractor's agreement that it will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The Contractor will take affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during employment, without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
perturbation.explanation: The Equal Employment Opportunity clause, which is a mandatory disclosure for government contractors, has been moved into the 'Entire Agreement' clause, making it less prominent and harder to find. This violates the spirit and potentially the letter of regulations requiring clear and conspicuous placement of such clauses.
perturbation.law_explanation: 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.4(a) mandates the inclusion of specific equal opportunity clauses in government contracts. Placing this clause within the 'Entire Agreement' section buries it, potentially obscuring it from the contractor's and employees' attention, thus hindering its effective implementation and enforcement as intended by the regulation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 63.214.10.252 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original contract text includes a clear and direct statement of Equal Employment Opportunity duties, as required by 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.4(a). The changed text buries this critical clause within the 'Entire Agreement' section. This placement obscures the clause, making it less prominent and harder to locate. This undermines the regulation's intent to ensure effective implementation and enforcement of equal opportunity. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both highlight this issue of obscuring the clause, thus diminishing its impact. The scraped law snippets are not directly relevant but provide context of the mandate.

perturbation.original_text: 2. AGREEMENT OF NON SEGREGATED FACILITIES In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8, Contractor agrees that it does not and will not maintain or provide for its employees any facilities segregated on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin at any of its establishments, and that it does not and will not permit its employees to perform their services at any location, under its control, where such segregated facilities are maintained. The term "facilities" as used herein means waiting rooms, work areas, restaurants and other eating areas, time clocks, rest rooms, wash rooms, locker rooms and other storage or dressing areas, parking lots, drinking fountains, recreation or entertainment areas, transportation, and housing facilities provided for employees; provided, that separate or single-user restroom and necessary dressing or sleeping areas shall be provided to assure privacy between the sexes.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. If, however, any provision of this Agreement is determined to be invalid or unenforceable, such invalidity or unenforceability shall not invalidate or render PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Not for use or disclosure outside SBC or Amdocs except under written agreement. <PAGE> 106 -79- unenforceable the entire Agreement, but rather the entire Agreement shall be construed as if not containing the particular invalid or unenforceable provision or provisions, and the rights and obligations of the parties shall be construed and enforced accordingly. In addition, the parties hereby agree to co-operate with each other to replace the invalid or unenforceable provision(s) with a valid and enforceable provision(s) which will achieve the same result (to the maximum legal extent) as the provision(s) determined to be invalid or unenforceable. AGREEMENT OF NON SEGREGATED FACILITIES In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8, Contractor agrees that it does not and will not maintain or provide for its employees any facilities segregated on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin at any of its establishments, and that it does not and will not permit its employees to perform their services at any location, under its control, where such segregated facilities are maintained. The term "facilities" as used herein means waiting rooms, work areas, restaurants and other eating areas, time clocks, rest rooms, wash rooms, locker rooms and other storage or dressing areas, parking lots, drinking fountains, recreation or entertainment areas, transportation, and housing facilities provided for employees; provided, that separate or single-user restroom and necessary dressing or sleeping areas shall be provided to assure privacy between the sexes.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Agreement of Non-Segregated Facilities' has been inserted into the 'Severability' clause. This placement obscures the clause's importance and makes it less likely to be noticed by relevant parties (employees, etc.), undermining its intended effect.
perturbation.law_explanation: 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.8 requires contractors to ensure non-segregated facilities. Placing the agreement within the 'Severability' clause, which deals with the enforceability of the contract's individual provisions, weakens its practical impact, as it is no longer presented as a clear and distinct obligation, contradicting the intent of the regulation.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states an agreement of non-segregated facilities, referencing 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8, emphasizing its importance and visibility within the contract. The changed text buries this important agreement within a 'Severability' clause, effectively diminishing its visibility and impact. This undermines the intent of 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.8, as explained by the LLM Law Explanation, which states the placement weakens its practical impact. Although the law snippets are not directly helpful, the Law Explanation combined with the Perturbation Explanation and the original/changed text comparison clearly demonstrates a Structural Flaw creating a Legal Contradiction because a legally required disclosure is placed in an irrelevant section.

perturbation.original_text: 3. AGREEMENT OF AFFIRMATIVE ACTION PROGRAM Contractor agrees that it has developed and is maintaining an Affirmative Action Plan as required by 41 C.F.R. ss.60-1.4(b).
perturbation.changed_text: PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Not for use or disclosure outside SBC or Amdocs except under written agreement. <PAGE> 107 -80- [Annex To Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement] ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATIONS I have read and understand the Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement dated ___________ between AMDOCS INC. and ____________, and agree to be bound by all the provisions of that Agreement as if I were a party thereto. ___________________________________ Signature ___________________________________ Name ___________________________________ Employer ___________________________________ Title ___________________________________ Date AGREEMENT OF AFFIRMATIVE ACTION PROGRAM Contractor agrees that it has developed and is maintaining an Affirmative Action Plan as required by 41 C.F.R. ss.60-1.4(b).
perturbation.explanation: The 'Agreement of Affirmative Action Program' has been moved to the Annex section, which is associated with the NDA rather than the main contract's compliance section. This obscures the affirmative action obligation and lessens its discoverability.
perturbation.law_explanation: 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.4(b) requires contractors to develop and maintain an Affirmative Action Program. Moving this agreement to the annex, linked to the NDA, disconnects it from the main contract's compliance framework, reducing its visibility and impact, thereby contradicting the regulation's intent to ensure active affirmative action efforts.

If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the contractor agrees to maintain an Affirmative Action Plan as required by 41 C.F.R. ss.60-1.4(b). The changed text moves this agreement to an annex associated with a Non-Disclosure Agreement, effectively obscuring its visibility and potentially diminishing its enforceability within the overall contract. This constitutes a structural flaw, as defined in the instructions, because a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant section. The law explanation supports this by stating that this disconnects the affirmative action program from the main contract's compliance framework, reducing its impact. While the scraped law snippets do not directly address this specific situation, the perturbation explanation and law explanation, combined with the change in contract structure, strongly suggest a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. AFFIRMATIVE ACTION FOR HANDICAPPED PERSONS AND DISABLED VETERANS, VETERANS OF THE VIETNAM ERA. In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-250.20 and 41 C.F.R.ss.60-741.20, the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by those provisions to be made a part of government contracts and subcontracts.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement shall be governed and construed under the laws of the State of New York, USA without giving effect to its provisions regarding conflicts of law. AFFIRMATIVE ACTION FOR HANDICAPPED PERSONS AND DISABLED VETERANS, VETERANS OF THE VIETNAM ERA. In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-250.20 and 41 C.F.R.ss.60-741.20, the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by those provisions to be made a part of government contracts and subcontracts.
perturbation.explanation: The Affirmative Action clause for handicapped persons and veterans has been relocated to the 'Governing Law' section. This misplacement reduces the likelihood that those responsible for implementing affirmative action will readily find and understand their obligations under these regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 41 C.F.R. § 60-741.20 mandates affirmative action program requirements for disabled veterans. Moving this clause into the 'Governing Law' section dilutes its prominence and makes it less accessible to those responsible for implementing and overseeing affirmative action, undermining the regulation's purpose of promoting employment opportunities for protected veterans.

According to the perturbation explanation and law explanation, this misplacement reduces the likelihood that those responsible for implementing affirmative action will readily find and understand their obligations, hindering the effective implementation of 41 C.F.R. § 60-741.20. The structural flaw directly contradicts the purpose of the affirmative action requirement by reducing its prominence.
